chapters.notes: Luke prologue, risen Jesus, Spirit promise, kingdom question, witness from Jerusalem to end of earth, ascension, upper room prayer with women/Mary/brothers, Judas/Scripture, Matthias.

Pentecost, rushing breath/wind, tongues as heard languages, Spirit poured on all flesh, Peter's Joel/Psalm speech, raised Jesus, turning/realignment, baptism, release, holy Spirit gift, shared community life.
Temple prayer, Beautiful Gate healing, name of Jesus Messiah, walking/leaping/praising, Peter's Solomon Portico witness, Author of life, complete wholeness, turning and times of restoration.

Arrest, council, name and saving/healing witness, rejected stone, no other name, boldness, prayer with Psalm 2, place shaken, Spirit-filled speech, one heart and soul/life, shared possessions, Barnabas.

Ananias/Sapphira as heart-truth rupture, awe, signs through apostolic hands, healing, public custody, messenger release, temple teaching, council, obey Divine rather than humans, Gamaliel caution, rejoicing under dishonor for the Name.
Hellenist/Hebrew widow-service conflict, seven Spirit-and-wisdom-filled servants appointed, Stephen full of grace and power, synagogue dispute, false witnesses, angelic face before council.

Stephen's Israel-history witness from Abraham to Joseph to Moses to temple critique, Most High not contained in handmade houses, resistance to holy Spirit, Son of Humanity vision, Stephen's death, Saul introduced.

Saul persecution and scattering, Philip in Samaria, healing/release and city joy, Simon Magus and heart correction, Peter/John Spirit reception, Ethiopian eunuch, Isaiah guidance, baptism, Spirit-snatching Philip, Caesarea route.

Saul encounter on Damascus road, Ananias as bridge, scales/sight/baptism, Damascus proclamation, basket escape, Barnabas mediation in Jerusalem, assembly peace, Peter heals Aeneas, raises Tabitha/Dorcas, Simon the tanner.

Cornelius vision, Peter rooftop vision, what the Divine cleansed do not make common, call no human common or unclean, no partiality, Gentile household hears, holy Spirit falls, baptism in Jesus Messiah's name.

Peter explains Gentile table fellowship in Jerusalem, Spirit says make no distinction, who was I to hinder the Divine, turning into life for nations, Antioch mission, Barnabas seeks Saul, Christian naming, Agabus famine relief.

Herod persecution, James killed, Peter imprisoned during Unleavened Bread, earnest assembly prayer, messenger release, Rhoda at Mary's house, Herod's judgment, word grows and multiplies, Barnabas/Saul return with John Mark.

Antioch prophets/teachers, Spirit sending of Barnabas/Saul, Cyprus, Elymas and Sergius Paulus, Pisidian Antioch synagogue speech, Davidic promise, resurrection, release and justification, Gentile joy, light to nations, opposition.

Iconium trust/opposition, Lystra healing, Zeus/Hermes misunderstanding, living Divine speech, Paul stoned and raised, strengthening disciples through pressures, appointing elders, door of trust to nations.
Jerusalem council, circumcision dispute, Peter no-distinction speech, James and David's tent, Gentile letter, it seemed good to holy Spirit and us, Judas/Silas encouragement, Paul/Barnabas split.

Timothy joins, Jerusalem decrees delivered, Spirit travel direction, Macedonian call, Lydia's opened heart and household, enslaved divination girl released, profit-loss opposition, Philippi prison, midnight prayer/hymns, earthquake, jailer household, Roman-citizen justice.

Thessalonica synagogue witness, Berean Scripture examination, Athens provocation, Areopagus speech, unknown god, Maker not in handmade temples, Divine nearness, poets, resurrection response, Dionysius and Damaris.
Corinth, Priscilla/Aquila, tentmaking, synagogue opposition, Crispus, night vision, Gallio, Cenchreae vow, Ephesus, Apollos instructed more accurately by Priscilla and Aquila.
Ephesian disciples, holy Spirit reception, Tyrannus hall, unusual powers, sons of Sceva and borrowed-name failure, costly scrolls burned, Artemis economy riot.

Macedonia/Greece, Troas bread-breaking, Eutychus restored, travel itinerary, Miletus farewell, serving with tears, Spirit-bound Jerusalem journey, guarding the flock, help the weak, more blessed to give.

Journey to Jerusalem, Tyre warning through Spirit, Philip's four prophesying daughters, Agabus binding prophecy, Paul ready for the name, James/elders, Law-sensitive purification, temple arrest, Roman rescue, Hebrew address setup.

Paul's Hebrew defense, shared Judean formation, Damascus encounter retold, Ananias as Law-devout witness, baptism/name, temple vision, Stephen accountability, nations commission, crowd reaction, Roman citizenship.
Sanhedrin conflict, high priest strike, resurrection hope, Pharisee/Sadducee fracture, Lord's night encouragement toward Rome, assassination plot, nephew warning, transfer to Felix.

Tertullus accusation, Paul defense before Felix, the Way as service to ancestral Divine, resurrection hope, conscience, absent accusers, Felix delay, Drusilla hearing, justice/self-control/coming judgment, bribe expectation, two-year custody.

translation_notes: Acts continues Luke's second volume and should preserve ordered narrative, Spirit agency, witness, prayer, communal life, and expansion from Jerusalem to nations.
Pneuma hagion should usually be holy Spirit, preserving Spirit agency rather than abstraction.
Martys/martyria should be witness/testimony, preserving public embodied witness.
Ekklesia should be assembly/community according to context, not automatically institutional church.
Metanoia should remain turning/realignment, and aphesis release/forgiveness according to context.
Sozo should remain saved/healed where rescue and embodied healing overlap.
Name language should preserve power/authority/invocation/resonance around the name of Jesus Messiah.
Keep Jewish temple, synagogue, prayer, festival, Scripture, and apostolic witness context intact; avoid replacement framing.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.

Acts 1-4 establishes the book's core template: Spirit promise, witness to the earth's edge, Pentecost speech, turning/release, communal sharing, temple healing, name-power, council opposition, boldness prayer, and Spirit-filled witness.

Acts 5-8 preserves the assembly's inner integrity crisis, apostolic opposition, practical justice for widows, Stephen's prophetic witness, Spirit resistance critique, Saul's violent introduction, Samaria's joy, correction of power-for-money distortion, and the Ethiopian eunuch as boundary-crossing Scripture-guided inclusion.

Acts 9-12 preserves Saul's reversal as name-bearing vocation, Ananias/Barnabas as bridge figures, Peter's healing/raising ministry, Gentile inclusion through Cornelius without partiality, Spirit falling before gatekeeping, Antioch's mixed mission, famine service, and Peter's prison release through prayer and messenger intervention.

Acts 13-16 preserves Spirit-sent mission from Antioch, synagogue-first witness, Gentile joy and opposition, living Divine proclamation in pagan context, Jerusalem council discernment, no-distinction grace, minimal table fellowship requirements, Spirit-directed travel, Lydia's opened heart, release from exploited divination, and Philippi prison praise/liberation.

Acts 17-20 preserves city-by-city mission through synagogue, marketplace, philosophical, domestic, and civic spaces; Divine nearness in Athens; Priscilla/Aquila as wisdom teachers; Spirit reception in Ephesus; name-power not as technique; economic backlash to the Way; and Paul's tearful shepherding farewell.

Acts 21-24 preserves the Jerusalem custody arc: Spirit warnings without coercion, prophesying daughters, Agabus sign-act, Law-sensitive witness, temple rumor, Roman rescue, Paul's accountability and nations commission, resurrection hope as central dispute, Rome-bound encouragement, and Felix's delayed compromised hearing.
next_actions: Complete Acts 25-28: Festus, appeal to Caesar, Agrippa/Bernice, Paul defense, ship voyage, storm, Malta, Rome arrival, final witness in Rome.

Add glossary entries or reuse hooks: pneuma hagion, martys, martyria, dynamis, onoma/name, ekklesia, metanoia, aphesis, sozo, koinonia, parresia/boldness, diakonia, episkope, ethnē/nations, charis/grace, hodos/Way, and episkopos/overseer.
